page: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor. This server features 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with 2 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory running at 7 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of memory per core. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8). There is no local NVMe storage or bundled GPU, and the baseline network bandwidth is rated at 0 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks reveal a strong contrast between single-core and multi-core capabilities. The instance demonstrates strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2056.0 and efficient single-threaded operations, including gzip compression at 33.85 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 14.22 GB/sec. However, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are significantly limited. The stress-ng multi-core CPU performance is categorized as poor, and Geekbench multi-core scores fall into the bottom 25% at 2628.0. Memory bandwidth is also constrained, with large-block read-only bandwidth measuring 19.96 GB/sec and write bandwidth at 14.37 GB/sec.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the m8i-flex.large offers an entry-level entry point into the Intel Gen8 architecture, providing modern DDR5 memory and advanced instruction sets without the premium cost of high-core instances. However, the lack of local storage and low multi-core scaling represent clear tradeoffs. This instance is suited for lightweight, single-threaded workloads, basic text processing, and small-scale application hosting where high memory bandwidth and parallel processing are not required.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 2 vCPUs, 1 physical core, and 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory. Operating at 3.9 GHz, this x86_64 server utilizes the Nitro hypervisor and includes advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. While single-core benchmarks are strong, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2056.0 and high single-threaded compression speeds, multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are comparatively weak. The instance lacks local storage and has a 0 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. It represents a cost-efficient option for entry-level workloads that rely on single-threaded execution, such as basic text processing, lightweight web serving, or small-scale development tasks, rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i-flex.large is a general-purpose x86_64 instance with 2 vCPUs, 8 GB DDR5 RAM, and strong single-core performance, suited for lightweight single-threaded workloads.

tagline: A general-purpose x86_64 instance featuring DDR5 memory and strong single-threaded performance for entry-level, compute-light workloads.
